element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cardinal direction navigation in tile-based dungeons
Learning character action options and party roles
Rotating elemental slots in battle formation; understanding spirit switching mechanics
Managing expensive magical resources; learning spell selection strategy
Recognizing boss tile placement; preparing for sudden high-threat battles
Managing spirit healer services; learning resurrection spell at high levels

element_introduction_order.element: Game over condition (any non-elemental character death)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Understanding party vulnerability; strategic retreat to towns

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Unpredictable spikes. Exploration-driven tension with sudden escalation via hidden boss placement. Relief cycles tied to town access rather than narrative beats. High penalty for failure creates sustained dread between save points.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Dead ends in labyrinthine dungeons; hidden dangers reward exploration but signal optional/trap paths through convoluted passage layout
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Boss characters placed on specific tiles without visual forewarning; discovery through movement rather than spatial cues

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate. Introduction to first-person navigation and basic turn combat. Early elemental spirits available. Minimal forewarning of threats.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating. Hidden boss encounters increase. Spirit management becomes critical. Inventory constraints tighten. Distance from save points grows.
difficulty_curve.late_game: High. Boss frequency increases. Party vulnerability (Rooks as critical resource) demands strategic play. Limited save access forces long dungeon commitments.
notable_spikes: First unexpected boss encounter (sudden threat revelation)
Mid-game dungeon length increases (distance from town grows)
Late-game elemental spirit coordination demands (multiple spirit deaths possible)

notable_level_patterns.description: Tile-based dungeons with frequent dead ends, convoluted passages, and limited visibility (only immediate surroundings visible). Designs compensate for low animation detail through intricate layout artwork.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic dungeon structure (specific level names not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: Boss characters placed on specific dungeon tiles without visual distinction. Discovery occurs through movement interaction, creating sudden battle escalation with little forewarning.
Each level contains a town area serving as the only save point. Town access becomes both goal and resource constraint, forcing strategic retreat decisions.

sources_used: wikipedia: Arcana (video game) - Gameplay section, Plot section, Character descriptions
